provisions.description: Prohibits reprogramming, transfer of funds, or reimbursements (including Economy Act and USDA Organic Act authorities) that create new programs, eliminate programs, increase funds for denied/restricted projects, relocate offices, reorganize, or contract out federal functions, without 30-day advance written approval from Appropriations Committees. Also restricts reprogramming exceeding $500,000 or 10% (whichever is less) that augments or reduces existing programs by 10%, and prohibits implementing new programs not carried out in the prior FY unless funded by this or another Act. Also restricts modifying major capital investments over $500,000 or 10%, establishing new organizational entities with 5+ personnel, and carrying out activities not in the budget request, without 30-day notice.
provisions.title: VII
provisions.raw_text: SEC. 716. (a) None of the funds provided by this Act, or provided by previous appropriations Acts to the agencies funded by this Act that remain avail
notes: Multi-subsection provision (a) through (e)
Subsection (a): prohibits reprogramming for new programs, eliminating programs, increasing denied funds, relocating offices, reorganizing, or contracting out without 30-day approval
Subsection (b): restricts reprogramming over $500,000 or 10% that augments, reduces by 10%, or changes from general savings without 30-day approval
Subsection (c): requires approval before implementing programs not carried out in prior FY unless funded by this or other Act
Subsection (d): restricts modifying capital investments over $500,000/10%, establishing new entities with 5+ personnel, or carrying out activities not in budget request without 30-day notice
Subsection (e): requires written or email confirmation of receipt of notification

provisions.description: Restricts reprogramming of funds provided in Title I and prior year appropriations, establishing specific thresholds for Investigations (25%/$150K), Construction (15%/$3M), Operation and Maintenance (15%/$5M with unlimited emergency authority), Mississippi River and Tributaries (follows respective account thresholds), and FUSRAP (15% of receiving project). Requires prior Appropriations Committee approval for creating/eliminating programs, redirecting directed funds, or exceeding thresholds. Sets $50,000 de minimis submission threshold. Exempts continuing authorities program from new program restriction.
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text: None of the funds provided in title I of this Act, or provided by previous appropriations Acts to the agencies or entities funded in title I of this Ac
notes: Covers subsections (a) through (c)
Subsection (a): main reprogramming restriction with 10 enumerated categories
Subsection (b): de minimis threshold of $50,000 for submissions to Appropriations Committees
Subsection (c): continuing authorities program exempt from (a)(1) new program restriction
Investigations (a)(6): 25% up to $150,000 for base >$100,000; $25,000 limit for base <$100,000; $25,000 for continuing studies
Construction (a)(7): 15% up to $3,000,000 for base >$2,000,000; $300,000 for base <$2,000,000; $3,000,000 for settled claims/changed conditions; $300,000 for continuing studies
O&M (a)(8): unlimited for emergencies (with notification); 15% up to $5,000,000 for base >$1,000,000; $150,000 for base <$1,000,000; $150,000 for continuing studies
MR&T (a)(9): follows respective account thresholds from (6)-(8)
FUSRAP (a)(10): 15% of receiving project base

notes: Heading: OUTER CONTINENTAL SHELF INSPECTION FEES

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of the Interior and Secretary of Agriculture to allocate, within 45 days of enactment, amounts from the National Parks and Public Land Legacy Restoration Fund and the Land and Water Conservation Fund for FY2024 as specified in the explanatory statement. Limits presidential reallocation authority. Establishes rules for contingency fund reallocations for unforeseen cost overruns. Requires submission of project data sheets and supplementary allocation lists concurrent with the FY2025 budget. Requires quarterly reports on fund balances.
deadlines: 45 days after enactment for NPPLLRF allocation
45 days after enactment for LWCF allocation
30 days before contingency reallocation actions exceeding 10%
Concurrent with FY2025 budget submission for project data sheets
provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 430. 
(a) 
(1) Within 45 days of enactment of this Act, the Secretary of the Interior shall allocate amounts made available from the National Par
notes: Subsection (a)(1) directs allocation of National Parks and Public Land Legacy Restoration Fund amounts per explanatory statement table
Subsection (a)(2) directs allocation of LWCF amounts per explanatory statement table
Subsection (b) limits presidential allocation authority to amounts specified by Congress, with proviso for continuing administration
Subsection (c) authorizes reallocation from Contingency Fund lines for unforeseen cost overruns within original scope, with notification requirements
Subsection (d)(1) requires NPPLLRF project data sheets with FY2025 budget submission including Contingency Fund line
Subsection (d)(2) requires LWCF supplementary allocation list totaling at least half the amount per agency, with willing seller and appraisal requirements
Subsection (e) requires quarterly status reports on committed, uncommitted, and unobligated balances

provisions.description: Establishes the methodology for distribution of the obligation limitation for Federal-aid highways for fiscal year 2024, including five-step distribution process, twelve categories of exceptions, redistribution of unused authority after August 1, applicability to transportation research programs, and redistribution of authorized funds not allocable due to the limitation.
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 120. (a) For fiscal year 2024, the Secretary of Transportation shall—
(1) not distribute from the obligation limitation for Federal-aid highways—
notes: Subsection (a) establishes five-step distribution methodology: (1) exclude administrative expenses and BTS, (2) exclude unobligated balances from prior years, (3) determine proportion, (4) distribute to Secretary-allocated programs, (5) distribute remainder to State-apportioned programs

Subsection (b) lists 12 categories of obligations exempt from the limitation, including emergency relief (sec. 125), various legacy programs, and $639,000,000 annually for the national highway performance program (sec. 119) for each of FY2013-2024
Subsection (c) requires redistribution of unused obligation authority after August 1, prioritizing States with large unobligated balances
Subsection (d) applies obligation limitation to research programs under 23 U.S.C. chapter 5, FAST Act title VI, and IIJA title III with 4-year availability
Subsection (e) requires redistribution of funds that will not be allocated due to limitation within 30 days, available for purposes described in 23 U.S.C. 133(b)

cross_references.description: Administrative expenses and programs excluded from distribution
National highway performance program; $639M annual exemption through FY2024
Emergency relief program exempt from obligation limitation
Surface transportation block grant eligible purposes for redistributed funds
MAP-21 referenced for prior apportionment under section 144

provisions.description: Authorizes the Secretary of HUD, for fiscal years 2024 and 2025, to transfer some or all project-based assistance, FHA-insured or HUD-held debt, and statutorily required low-income/very-low-income use restrictions from one or more multifamily housing projects to another, subject to detailed conditions including: no reduction in number/configuration of occupied low-income units; transferring project must be physically obsolete or economically nonviable; receiving project must meet physical standards; tenant notification and consultation required; tenants need not vacate until new units are available; transfer must be in tenants' best interest; subordination of liens to FHA mortgages; continuation of use agreements; and no increased FHA mortgage cost absent appropriations. Phased transfers are permitted. Secretary must conduct an evaluation of the transfer authority.

provisions.description: Requires entities receiving housing assistance payments to maintain decent, safe, and sanitary conditions. Establishes enforcement framework for multifamily projects with section 8 contracts receiving failing UPCS scores or failing to certify correction of exigent health and safety deficiencies within 3 days. Enforcement actions include Notice of Default, replacement of management, civil money penalties, contract abatement, ownership transfer, exclusionary sanctions, judicial receivership, and other remedies. Requires the Secretary to maintain project-based contracts and, if infeasible, to provide alternative rental assistance.

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to conduct a study on the effectiveness of the Veterans suicide hotline during the 5-year period beginning January 1, 2016, analyzing national suicide data and hotline data to determine the number of veterans receiving follow-up services, the number not referred to or not continuing mental health care who commit suicide, and the number who commit or attempt suicide.

provisions.description: Authorizes the use of Medical Services account funds to provide fertility counseling and treatment using assisted reproductive technology to a covered veteran or spouse of a covered veteran, and adoption reimbursement to a covered veteran. A covered veteran is one with a service-connected disability resulting in inability to procreate without fertility treatment.
provisions.policy_area: Veterans health care — fertility treatment and adoption
provisions.title: II
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 234. 
(a) Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the amounts appropriated or otherwise made available to the Department of Veterans Affairs f
notes: Subsection (a) authorizes two uses: (1) fertility counseling/treatment using assisted reproductive technology for covered veterans or their spouses, and (2) adoption reimbursement for covered veterans.
Subsection (b) defines 'service-connected', 'covered veteran', 'assisted reproductive technology', and 'adoption reimbursement'.

Assisted reproductive technology defined by reference to DoD policy memorandum of April 3, 2012, with modifications: (A) time limits on embryo cryopreservation/storage removed, (B) embryo cryopreservation/storage allowed without duration limitation.
Adoption reimbursement follows DoD adoption reimbursement program terms under DoD Instruction 1341.09.
Subsection (c) subjects these funds to requirements in section 508 of division H of P.L. 115-141 (Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2018).
'Notwithstanding any other provision of law' language overrides other restrictions.

provisions.description: Amends the Rental Assistance Demonstration (RAD) language from the 2012 HUD Appropriations Act with seven changes: (1) adds 'Public Housing Fund' to eligible fund sources; (2) extends deadline from 2024 to 2029; (3) adds provisos allowing conversion of tenant protection voucher assistance to project-based subsidy contracts at partially converting properties, and allowing transfer of funds between Tenant-Based and Project-Based Rental Assistance headings; (4) adds 'Public Housing Fund', 'Self-Sufficiency Programs', and 'Family Self-Sufficiency' headings and ongoing services availability language; (5) adds eligibility for senior preservation rental assistance contract conversions under section 811 of AHEOA 2000; (6) adds section 811 AHEOA 2000 reference; (7) broadens conversion language to include section 202(c)(2), section 811 AHEOA 2000, and section 811(d)(2) of Cranston-Gonzalez.

notes: Seven separate amendments to RAD language
Paragraph (1) adds Public Housing Fund as eligible source
Paragraph (2) extends RAD deadline from 2024 to 2029
Paragraph (3) adds multiple provisos for partial conversions, tenant protection voucher conversions, and fund transfers between TBRA and PBRA
Paragraph (4) adds Public Housing Fund, Self-Sufficiency Programs, Family Self-Sufficiency, and ongoing services language
Paragraph (5) adds eligibility for senior preservation rental assistance contract conversions under AHEOA 2000
Paragraph (6) adds AHEOA 2000 section 811 reference alongside Housing Act of 1959
Paragraph (7) broadens conversion language to three statutory sources

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of HHS to annually link, analyze, and publish on a publicly available website T-MSIS data on substance use disorder and mental health services for Medicaid and CHIP enrollees, disaggregated by age and State, with extensive data elements including diagnoses, service utilization, managed care vs. fee-for-service breakdowns, readmission rates, emergency department visits post-discharge, assessments, and prescription data. Establishes timelines for initial publication (18 months after enactment), annual updates, revised research-ready publications (within 3 years), and data sharing with researchers (by January 1, 2025).
deadlines: 18 months after enactment for first analysis
January 1 of each calendar year for annual updates
3 years after enactment for revised research-ready publication
January 1, 2025 for initiation of data-sharing activities

notes: Subsection (a) makes permanent the IMD state plan amendment option that was set to expire September 30, 2023

Subsection (b) revises maintenance of effort requirements, giving states option to use FY2018 or most recently ended FY as baseline; includes 1-year transition for states with amendments in effect on September 30, 2023

Subsection (c)(1) adds new requirements for evidence-based placement criteria, utilization management, and review process for IMD compliance with program standards, effective for calendar quarters beginning on or after October 1, 2025
Subsection (c)(2) adds one-time assessment requirement for treatment availability by level of care and region, to commence within 12 months of amendment approval
Subsection (c)(3) clarifies levels of care definitions

Subsection (d) allows states with amendments in effect on September 30, 2023 to renew with effective date of October 1, 2023, notwithstanding 42 CFR 430.20, if request submitted within 60 days of enactment

provisions.description: Prohibits States from using grant funds awarded under SEC. 206(a) to provide medical assistance or child health assistance or pregnancy-related assistance to individuals, or to build prisons, jails, or other carceral facilities, except for improvements directly and primarily for meeting health care needs of eligible incarcerated individuals.
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text: A State shall not use funds from a grant awarded under this subsection to—
notes: Prohibits direct provision of medical assistance, child health assistance, or pregnancy-related assistance
Prohibits construction of prisons/jails/carceral facilities except health-care-related improvements for eligible incarcerated individuals

provisions.description: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to issue detailed guidance to States addressing common implementation and operational challenges in ensuring access to authorized care before, during, and after incarceration for Medicaid/CHIP-eligible individuals.
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text: Not later than 18 months after the date of enactment of this Act, the Secretary shall issue detailed guidance to States that addresses common implement
notes: Guidance must address challenges in complying with enrollment continuity requirements, pre-release services requirements, and State plan options

Must include best practices for: eligibility/enrollment modifications, federal financial participation availability, screening and application assistance, timely medication supply upon release, and community-based provider networks

provisions.program_name: Certified community behavioral health clinic services under Medicaid
provisions.statutory_reference: Section 1905 of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396d)
provisions.title: I
provisions.raw_text:
Section 1905 of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396d) is amended— (1) in subsection (a)—
(A) in paragraph (30), by striking ''; and'' and insertin
notes: Amends definition of medical assistance under Medicaid (Section 1905) to add certified community behavioral health clinic (CCBHC) services as a new benefit category
Adds new paragraph (31) to subsection (a) and new subsection (jj) defining CCBHC services and CCBHC certification criteria
Subsection (b) provides effective date: amendments apply to medical assistance furnished on or after date of enactment

CCBHC services include crisis mental health services, screening/assessment/diagnosis, treatment planning, outpatient mental health/SUD services, primary care screening, intensive case management, psychiatric rehabilitation, peer support, and care for armed forces members/veterans
CCBHCs must be certified by a State as meeting criteria established by the Secretary under section 223 of the Protecting Access to Medicare Act as of January 1, 2024

cross_references.description: Adds new paragraph (31) and new subsection (jj)

provisions.description: Requires that any amendment to, change to, or termination of the 2023 Amended U.S.-FSM Compact, 2023 Amended U.S.-RMI Compact, or U.S.-Palau Compact (by mutual agreement or unilateral U.S. action) shall not enter into force until Congress has incorporated it into an Act of Congress. Extends this requirement to actions under sections 431, 441, or 442 of the compacts, and to amendments or termination of certain specified subsidiary agreements.

provisions.description: Prohibits use of funds for research commencing after enactment using canines, felines, or non-human primates unless the Secretary of Veterans Affairs personally certifies in writing that no alternatives exist, objectives are combat-related, and ethical considerations are addressed. Requires extensive reporting, voluntary USDA inspections, and a plan to eliminate such research within 2 years.
provisions.title: II
provisions.raw_text: (a) None of the funds appropriated or otherwise made available by this Act may be used to conduct research commencing on or after the date of enactment
notes: Subsection (a): Blanket prohibition on new canine/feline/non-human primate research unless Secretary approves

Subsection (b): Secretary must personally certify four criteria: (A) no alternative models, (B) combat-related per 10 U.S.C. 1413(e), (C) consistent with Dec 15 2017 VA policy, (D) ethical considerations. Authority may not be delegated.
Subsection (c): 30-day pre-commencement report to Appropriations Committees on approved research
Subsection (d): Biannual report starting 180 days after enactment on ongoing animal research
Subsection (e): Annual submission to voluntary USDA inspections starting 180 days after enactment
Subsection (f): Annual report on Animal Welfare Act violations starting 180 days after enactment
Subsection (g): Plan to eliminate canine, feline, and non-human primate research within 2 years of enactment

provisions.description: Establishes requirements for the 3 United States appointees to the Joint Economic Management Committee under section 213 of the 2023 Amended U.S.-FSM Compact. Appointees shall be voting members and Federal employees, appointed for 2-year terms (with up to 2 reappointments) by the Secretary of State, Secretary of the Interior, and the Interagency Group on Freely Associated States. Requires specific qualifications in accounting, auditing, budget analysis, compliance, grant administration, program management, or international economics. Requires notification to Congress within 90 days of appointment. Requires submission of Committee reports to Congress within 90 days and notice of timeliness of FSM report submissions.
deadlines: 90 days after Committee receives or completes a report
90 days after FSM submits a required report

Members shall be Federal employees, appointed for up to 5-year terms (with up to 2 additional 2-year reappointments) by the Secretary of State, Secretary of the Interior, and Secretary of the Treasury. At least 2 members must have specific qualifications. Requires notification to Congress within 90 days of appointment and submission of Committee reports within 90 days.

Reaffirms the return-of-unexpended-funds mechanism and the Fund Manager's role in investing retained funds to continue health services after the initial compact period.

Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to provide grants to the Government of the RMI for a planting and agricultural maintenance program on Bikini, Enewetak, Rongelap, and Utrik. Authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to provide, without reimbursement, food programs to the people of the RMI.

provisions.description: Amends Section 1724 of title 38, United States Code, to authorize the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to furnish hospital care and medical services in the Freely Associated States (Federated States of Micronesia, Republic of the Marshall Islands, and Republic of Palau) to eligible veterans, subject to agreements with the governments of the Freely Associated States. Care may be furnished through contracts, reimbursement, or direct provision by VA health care personnel, for any condition regardless of service connection. Includes provisions for continued eligibility if a country ceases to be a Freely Associated State.

provisions.description: Amends Sections 402, 403, and 431 of the Personal Responsibility and Work Opportunity Reconciliation Act of 1996 (PRWORA) to make citizens of the Freely Associated States who lawfully reside in the U.S. eligible for all specified federal programs (not just Medicaid), by: (1) creating an exception from the bar on benefits for qualified aliens; (2) removing the limitation to Medicaid from the 5-year wait exception; and (3) expanding the qualified alien definition to cover all designated federal programs.

SEC. 209(f)(3) amends 8 U.S.C. 1641 to expand qualified alien definition beyond Medicaid only
